body.title: [sprint] Batch-harvest a distinction-bearing stuck corpus + seal an eval slice
body.description:
**Problem (the discovered chain gap):** the defeating-question classifier (T-ds-defeating-question-classifier) must grade against a HELD-OUT slice of REAL stuck-transcripts spanning BOTH classes it exists to separate: (a) DESIGN-STUCK — the spec didn't say, the builder flails for lack of intent (the gold, sortable into the tree); (b) CAPABILITY-STUCK — well-specified but the fixed tier simply can't. The first live harvest run (T-ds-first-harvest-run, 2026-07-06) produced exactly ONE transcript, and it is pure capability-stuck (a fully-specified Raft ticket devstral can't execute) at max_turns=4 — turn-starvation blurs the two classes by construction. No batch-harvest step exists; the classifier body ASSUMED tickets 1+2 generate the corpus, but 1+2 were the machine — the corpus comes from RUNNING harvest over varied seeds, which has not happened. Grading a design-vs-capability classifier against a corpus that contains only capability-stuck (or against author-invented fixtures) is the self-consistency trap the classifier's own eval-grounding forbids.

**Proposed shape:** a batch operator runner (extend devlab/claudecode/harvest_run.py or a sibling harvest_batch.py) that runs harvest-mode at a REALISTIC cap (~12-20 turns, NOT 4 — high enough that design-stuck exhibits its flailing signature and capability-stuck exhibits genuine can't-execute, so the transcript CONTAINS the distinction) over a curated seed set: K deliberately UNDER-SPECIFIED tickets (the weekend-architect archetype — a vague ask a good spec would resolve) to induce design-stuck, and K WELL-SPECIFIED-BUT-HARD tickets to induce capability-stuck. Each run: isolated cwd scratch (CP6), pre-warmed, route-guarded to free Hex ollama ($0), transcript lands in io_corpus correlated by ticket_id with role/turn (already works, T-corpus-visibility-gaps). After the batch, SEAL a held-out slice of those real transcripts via eval_slice.py EvalSlice(name, budget).seal(entries) — the reality-uncoupled eval surface the classifier reads (budgeted, logged). The sealed slice + manifest is the deliverable, ready for (a) Akien's human-label pass and (b) the classifier quality-eval.

**Seed curation is the crux (needs-deep-smell):** a design-stuck seed must be genuinely under-specified (missing intent a spec would carry), NOT merely small — and a capability-stuck seed must be genuinely well-specified but beyond the fixed tier, NOT just large. If seeds don't cleanly span the two classes, the corpus is noise and the classifier eval is meaningless. Curate a first set of ~4 design-stuck + ~4 capability-stuck; label the SEED's intended class in the batch manifest (this is the seed-design intent, NOT the ground-truth label — the human pass over the transcripts is the label of record).

**Affected files:** new devlab/claudecode/harvest_batch.py (operator batch runner, reuses harvest_run.py's warm/route-guard/cwd/pin helpers); new seed-set fixture (e.g. devlab/claudecode/harvest_seeds/*.json — curated seed tickets); consumes unseen_university/devices/inference/eval_slice.py (seal) + io_corpus (corpus_root/read). No production inference code changes — the harvest machine is already built and proven.

**Design rules:** inference-proxy-only (route through resolve_domain, never a direct model call); OOP-first for any stateful batch controller; docs-live-in-code on harvest_batch.py; route-guard each run to free Hex before trusting it as a real wall (a paid/mis-routed run is a routing artifact, not harvest data); log every run's ticket_id + wall class + turns; seal via eval_slice.py, never hand-roll the manifest; NO SQLITE — the corpus and slice are flat-file JSONL.

**Scope boundary:** curate seeds + run the batch + seal the slice + produce the manifest. NOT the classifier itself (next ticket) and NOT the human-label pass (Akien's, downstream of the sealed slice).

**Validation:** the sealed slice manifest lists >=1 transcript per class-intent, each joinable to an io_corpus record by ticket_id; eval_slice.manifest() returns a hash covering the sealed entries; a read-log entry appears per budgeted read.

**Observable via:** log.info('harvest_batch: seed=%s class_intent=%s wall=%s turns=%d ticket_id=%s') per run; log.info('harvest_batch: sealed slice=%s entries=%d hash=%s') at seal; the sealed manifest file on disk.

**Test plan:** (a) PLUMBING PROOF NODE (hermetic, stubbed domain.run returning canned per-seed transcripts, hand fixtures OK for plumbing): given a 2-seed set, the batch runner writes one io_corpus-shaped record per seed correlated by ticket_id AND seals a slice whose manifest hash covers exactly those entries — a hollow runner that seals an empty/partial slice fails the entry-count assert. red->green. (b) DISTINCTION-BEARING span: whether the REAL harvested corpus genuinely spans design-stuck and capability-stuck is a MEASUREMENT confirmed by Akien's human-label pass over the transcripts — report the class distribution; ship this half shipped-unproven naming the lever (the span is only real once a human labels the real transcripts) if the batch has not been human-labeled at close time.

**Completion criteria:** a sealed eval_slice exists on disk whose manifest hash covers >=1 real io_corpus transcript per class-intent (design-stuck + capability-stuck), each joinable by ticket_id; the batch runner has a green hermetic plumbing proof (per-seed record + slice-seal); the distinction-bearing span is reported as a class distribution and handed to Akien for the label pass.

**Cleanup:** the hermetic plumbing test writes corpus records + a sealed slice into tmp_path via the UU_INFERENCE_CORPUS + eval_slice root=tmp_path overrides (no real store touched); each run's scratch cwd is rm -rf'd (harvest_run.py pattern). The REAL batch's io_corpus records + sealed slice are intentional deliverables (the corpus), NOT test artifacts — they persist by design.

**Rollback:** no production inference code changes — harvest_batch.py + seed fixtures are new operator-only files under devlab/claudecode/. Rollback = delete the operator tool; the harvest machine and corpus/eval_slice primitives are untouched.

**audit-emphasis:** needs-deep-smell (L, seed curation is HIGH design risk — bad seeds make the whole downstream classifier eval meaningless).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that the defeating-question classifier is graded against real stuck-transcripts that genuinely span design-stuck and capability-stuck, so its verdict is uncoupled from the author who wrote the fixtures.
